chunks.text: Modern society is full of contradiction.
An inhabitant who has become materially wealthy feels only an inferior form of happiness in spirit.
Why?
With the spread of social media
we have come to relate to others more than ever before.
But at the same time, chances to compare others with ourselves have also increased.
The lives of others look attractive.
Beautiful travel destinations, delicious meals, impressive achievements.
Inevitably, our own life comes to feel dull.
But what social media shows is only a portion of another's life, and at that
a deliberately chosen moment — the most glittering one.
Everyone, behind the camera, hides the fatigue and worries of daily life.
Psychologist indicate that excessive social media use can become the basis for depression and anxiety.
Especially for the young, especially for teenagers, the consequence is serious.
The answer is not to quit social media entirely.
It is to keep distance and turn our eyes to our own life.
To step away from the folly of comparing another's peak with our own average.
True happiness is not found in the comparison with others
but in the heart that appreciates oneself.
To indeed realize this is the challenge of those of us who live in the modern age.
